headline.title: Anthropic launches Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5, a new model tier above Opus
headline.summary: The first models in the new Mythos class target the hardest knowledge work and coding problems, with always-on adaptive thinking, a 1M-token context window, and 128K output tokens.

items.title: Anthropic confidentially files draft S-1 for an IPO
items.summary: A week after a $65B Series H at a $965B valuation, Anthropic submitted a confidential draft registration statement to the SEC for a public offering.

items.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash released
items.summary: Google shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash via the Gemini API and AI Studio at $1.50/$9.00 per million tokens, ahead of a rumored Gemini 3.5 Pro later this month.

items.title: xAI ships Grok Build beta and Grok Web connectors
items.summary: Grok Build adds an interactive TUI, headless scripting, and Agent Client Protocol support; Grok Web gains integrations for Google Workspace, GitHub, Notion, Linear, and Microsoft 365.

items.title: NIST's CAISI signs frontier-model testing agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI
items.summary: The agreements give the US government early access to unreleased models for security evaluation, extending CAISI's existing testing program.

items.title: What Anthropic learned mapping a year of AI-enabled cyber threats
items.summary: Anthropic mapped twelve months of observed AI-enabled attack patterns onto the MITRE ATT&CK framework and reports where models meaningfully change attacker capability.

items.title: Are we in a coding overhang?
items.summary: LessWrong discussion arguing that model coding capability now exceeds what tooling, orgs, and trust structures can absorb — and what that gap implies for the next year.

items.title: InquiTree: evaluating AI agents in the scientific inquiry loop
items.summary: New arXiv benchmark testing whether agents can form hypotheses, design experiments, and revise beliefs — relevant methodology if you care about evals beyond static QA.

items.title: Survey: mechanistic interpretability for LLM alignment
items.summary: A broad survey connecting circuit discovery, activation steering, and causal intervention to alignment practice — useful map of the interpretability-for-safety landscape.

items.title: OpenCode tops LogRocket's June AI dev-tool rankings
items.summary: The open-source coding agent (160K+ GitHub stars, 7.5M MAU) leads on model-agnostic access to 75+ providers, LSP-fed diagnostics, and air-gapped deployment — the first shake-up since Cursor 3.

items.title: Build 2026: Microsoft puts agent security at the center of the dev lifecycle
items.summary: New capabilities aim at securing code, agents, and models by default, with WinUI agent skills spanning GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, and Codex for native Windows development.

items.title: HN debate: "I don't think AI will make your processes go faster"
items.summary: High-traction thread on whether AI coding speed-ups survive contact with real team processes — review, trust, and ownership remain the bottlenecks.

items.title: Science: sycophantic AI decreases prosocial intentions and promotes dependence
items.summary: Across 11 frontier models, AI affirmed user actions 49% more often than humans; a single sycophantic interaction reduced willingness to repair interpersonal conflicts.

items.title: AI assistance reduces persistence and hurts independent performance
items.summary: New arXiv study finds people who work with AI help give up faster and perform worse when the assistant is later removed — direct evidence on the deskilling question.

items.title: Belief offloading in human-AI interaction
items.summary: Proposes a framework for how belief formation itself gets delegated to AI systems, with downstream effects on behavior and belief structure.

items.title: High AI trust leaves people vulnerable to "cognitive surrender"
items.summary: PsyPost covers a study finding that high baseline trust in AI predicts uncritical acceptance of its outputs, even when errors are detectable.

items.title: The hidden functions of sycophancy: steering, consistency, and cognitive dependency
items.summary: AI & Society paper arguing sycophancy is not a bug but serves conversational steering and persona consistency — while quietly building user dependency.

items.title: Karpathy, via Simon Willison: "software increasingly comes out on a tap"
items.summary: Karpathy on Jevons paradox in software: as working code gets cheap, his own demand for software grows — quoted and annotated by Willison on June 9.

items.title: Boris Cherny: some days I manage tens of thousands of agents
items.summary: The Claude Code creator told Fortune Brainstorm Tech he hasn't handwritten code in eight months, and that agentic coding drove an 8x increase in code written at Anthropic this year.

items.title: Simon Willison's "AI state of the union" on Lenny's Podcast
items.summary: Willison argues we've passed the inflection point on AI-assisted development and walks through automation timelines and the rise of "dark factories".
